MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This Question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Number 19 and 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions, carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions, carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{D}{/tex} and {tex}{2}{/tex} questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{E}{/tex}.
  9. Use of calculator is not allowed.

21.

Find the value of {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\tan ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)+\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}(-2){/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\tan ^{-1}\left(-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)+\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}(-2){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{6}-\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}

22.(i)

If {tex}y=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^{2}{/tex}, then find {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 \sin ^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right)\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=4 y{/tex}
Differentiating with respect to '{tex}x{/tex}', we get
{tex} \left(1-x^2\right) 2 \frac{d y}{d x} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-2 x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=4 \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left(1-x^2\right) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}=2 {/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

If {tex}{y}^{{x}}={x}^{{y}}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}y^x=x^y \Rightarrow x \log y=y \log x{/tex}
Differentiating with respect to '{tex}x{/tex}',
{tex} \frac{x}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y=\frac{y}{x}+\frac{d y}{d x} \log x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}\left(\frac{y-x \log y}{x-y \log x}\right) {/tex}

23.

Given that {tex}f(x)=\frac{\log x}{x}{/tex}, find the point of local maximum of {tex}f(x){/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})=\frac{1-\log {x}}{{x}^{2}},{/tex}
{tex} \therefore {f}^{\prime}({x})=0 \Rightarrow \log {x}=1 \Rightarrow {x}={e}{/tex}
{tex}f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{2 x \log x-3 x}{x^{4}} \Rightarrow f^{\prime \prime}(e)=-\frac{1}{e^{3}} \lt 0{/tex} i.e. {tex}x=e{/tex} is a point of local maximum.

24.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}-1}{x^{3}-x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{x^3-1}{x^3-x} d x =\int\left(1+\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x+1}\right) d x {/tex}
{tex} =x+\log |x|-\log |x+1|+c {/tex}

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int_{-4}^{0}|x+2| d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int_{-4}^{0} |{x}+2 | {dx}=\int_{-4}^{-2}-({x}+2) {dx}+\int_{-2}^{0}({x}+2) {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\left.\left.=-\frac{(x+2)^{2}}{2}\right]_{-4}^{-2}+\frac{(x+2)^{2}}{2}\right]_{-2}^{0}=2+2=4{/tex}

25.

Find the angle between the lines
{tex}\frac{5-x}{-7}=\frac{y+2}{-5}=\frac{z}{1} \text { and } \frac{x}{1}=\frac{y}{2}=\frac{z}{3}{/tex}

Solution:

The direction ratios of the two lines are: 7, -5, 1 and 1, 2, 3 respectively.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The angle between the two lines is given by: {tex}\theta =\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{7(1)-5(2)+1(3)}{\sqrt{49+25+1} \sqrt{1+4+9}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \theta =\cos ^{-1} 0=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

26.(i)

Find a matrix A such that {tex}{A}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also, find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex} A =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} =-\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -5 \\ 2 & 8 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
Also, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{34}\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 5 \\ -2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Given a square matrix {tex}A{/tex} of order 3 such that {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, show that {tex}A^{3}=A^{-1}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}{A}^{{3}}={A}^{-1} \Leftrightarrow {A}^{4}={I}{/tex}
{tex}A^{4}=A^{2} A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=I{/tex}

27.(i)

If {tex}x \sin (a+y)-\sin y=0{/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} x=\frac{\sin y}{\sin (a+y)}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y) \cos y-\sin y \cos (a+y)}{\sin ^2(a+y)} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin a}{\sin ^2(a+y)}, {/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^2(a+y)}{\sin a} {/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}, if {tex}y=(\cos x)^{x}+\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{x}{/tex}.

Solution:

Let {tex}y=u+v \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
{tex} u=(\cos x)^x \Rightarrow \log u=x \log \cos x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=(\cos x)^x[\log \cos x-x \tan x] {/tex}
{tex} v=\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{x} \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=-\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{x} \sqrt{1-x}} {/tex}
{tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=(\cos x)^x[\log \cos x-x \tan x]-\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{x} \sqrt{1-x}} {/tex}

28.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{{dx}}{\cos {x} \sqrt{\cos 2 {x}}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{d x}{\cos x \sqrt{\cos 2 x}} =\int \frac{d x}{\cos x \sqrt{\cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x}} {/tex}
{tex} =\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{\sqrt{1-\tan ^2 x}} d x {/tex}
{tex} =\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-t^2}} d t, \text { using } \tan x=t, \sec ^2 x \cdot d x=d t {/tex}
{tex} =\sin ^{-1} t+c=\sin ^{-1}(\tan x)+c {/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{5 x-3}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^{2}}} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{5 x-3}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}} d x {/tex}{tex}=-\frac{5}{4} \int \frac{-4 x+4}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}} d x+\sqrt{2} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(\left(\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}\right)^2-(x-1)^2\right)}} d x {/tex}
{tex} =-\frac{5}{2} \sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}{/tex}{tex}+\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}(x-1)\right)+c {/tex}

29.

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}y d x-x d y+(x \log x) d x=0{/tex}

Solution:

The given differential equation can be written as, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{1}{x} y=\log x{/tex}
{tex} \therefore P=-\frac{1}{x}, Q=\log x {/tex}
{tex} \text { I.F. }=e^{\int-\frac{1}{x} d x}{/tex} {tex}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log \frac{1}{x}}=\frac{1}{x} {/tex}
The solution of the differential equation is:
{tex} y \cdot \frac{1}{x}=\int \log x \cdot \frac{1}{x} d x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=\frac{(\log x)^2}{2}+c, \text { or } y=\frac{1}{2} x \cdot(\log x)^2+c x {/tex}

30.

If the vectors {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} represent the three sides of a triangle, then show that {tex}\vec{{a}} \times \vec{{b}}=\vec{{b}} \times \vec{{c}}=\vec{{c}} \times \vec{{a}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex} \vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \vec{a} \times(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})=\vec{0} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{a}+\vec{a} \times \vec{b}+\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=\vec{0} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=-\vec{a} \times \vec{c},{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{a} {/tex}
Similarly, {tex}\vec{{b}} \times \vec{{c}}=\vec{{c}} \times \vec{{a}}{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \vec{{a}} \times \vec{{b}}=\vec{{b}} \times \vec{{c}}=\vec{{c}} \times \vec{{a}}{/tex}

31.

It is known that {tex}20 \%{/tex} of the students in a school have above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance and {tex}80 \%{/tex} of the students are irregular. Past year results show that {tex}80 \%{/tex} of students who have above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance and {tex}20 \%{/tex} of irregular students get '{tex}A{/tex}' grade in their annual examination. At the end of a year, a student is chosen at random from the school and is found to have an 'A' grade. What is the probability that the student is irregular?

Solution:

Let B : Student getting 'A' grade
{tex}{E}_1{/tex}: Student having above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance
{tex}{E}_2{/tex}: Student being irregular
{tex} P\left(E_1\right)= \frac{20}{100} ; {/tex}{tex}P\left(E_2\right)=\frac{80}{100} P\left(B \mid E_1\right)=\frac{80}{100} ; {/tex}{tex}P\left(B \mid E_2\right)=\frac{20}{100} {/tex}
{tex} P\left(E_2 \mid B\right) =\frac{P\left(E_2\right) P\left(B \mid E_2\right)}{P\left(E_1\right) P\left(B \mid E_1\right)+P\left(E_2\right) P\left(B \mid E_2\right)} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{0.8 \times 0.2}{0.2 \times 0.8+0.8 \times 0.2}=\frac{1}{2} {/tex}

Section - D

This section comprises Long Answer (LA) type questions of 5 marks each.
32.

Check whether the relation {tex}S{/tex} in the set of all real numbers {tex}(\mathbb{R}){/tex} defined by {tex}{S}=\left\{({a}, {b}): {a} \leq {b}^{3}\right\}{/tex} is reflexive, symmetric or transitive.

Solution:

Reflexive: Let {tex}\frac{1}{2} \nleq\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^3, {/tex}
{tex}\therefore {~S}{/tex} is not reflexive.
Symmetric: {tex}1 \leq 2^3 \Rightarrow(1,2) \in S{/tex} but {tex}2 \not \leq 1^3 \Rightarrow(2,1) \notin S . {/tex}
{tex}\therefore S{/tex} is not symmetric.
Transitive: {tex}10 \leq 6^3 \Rightarrow(10,6) \in S \& 6 \leq 2^3 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(6,2) \in S{/tex} but {tex}10 \not \leq 2^3 \Rightarrow(10,2) \notin S{/tex}
{tex} \therefore {S}{/tex} is not Transitive.

33.(i)

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the curve {tex}y=\sqrt{4-x^{2}}{/tex}, the lines {tex}x=-\sqrt{2}{/tex} and {tex}x=\sqrt{3}{/tex} and the {tex}x{/tex}-axis.

Solution:


Area of the region bounded by the curve
{tex} =\int\limits_{-\sqrt{2}}^{\sqrt{3}} \sqrt{4-x^2} d x {/tex}

If the given lines intersect then,
